perturbation.original_text: This Agree ment also applies to all users who are granted access to FAU-issued computing and technology resources (e.g., desktops, laptops, tablets, mobile phones, printers, etc.), application systems or access to the FAU netwo rk, whether the user is affiliated with FAU or not, and whether access to or use of the information systems occurs locally or from remote locations.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement also applies to all users who are granted access to FAU-issued computing and technology resources (e.g., desktops, laptops, tablets, mobile phones, printers, etc.), application systems or access to the FAU netwo rk, whether the user is affiliated with FAU or employed by FAU. This agreement does not apply to external users who use the FAU network.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by stating that the agreement applies to FAU employees and affiliated users, but does not apply to external users that use the FAU network. This contradicts the original text, that applied the agreement to all the users, regardless if they are employed or not. This contradicts later sections of the document that refer to all users. This change specifically contradicts the term user as it is now interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This information may include, but i s not limited to, patient health records, as well as information regarding human res ources, payroll, fiscal matters, research, and strategic pl anning, and may exist in any form, including electronic, video, spoken, or written. This Agreement applies to all members of the workforce, including but not limited to, employees, volunteers, students, faculty, physicians, and third parties, whether temporary or permanent, paid or not paid, visiting, or designat ed as affiliates or associates, who are employed by, contracted with, or under the direct control of FAU. This Agree ment also applies to all users who are granted access to FAU-issued computing and technology resources (e.g., desktops, laptops, tablets, mobile phones, printers, etc.), application systems or access to the FAU netwo rk, whether the user is affiliated with FAU or not, and whether access to or use of the information systems occurs locally or from remote locations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction. The original text applies the agreement to 'all users...whether the user is affiliated with FAU or not'. The changed text limits this by stating the agreement 'does not apply to external users who use the FAU network.' The contradicted text states the agreement applies to 'all users who are granted access to FAU-issued computing...whether the user is affiliated with FAU or not'. The changed text therefore contradicts this section of the document by excluding a subset of users (external users of the FAU network) from the agreement's scope, which was previously stated to apply to all users regardless of affiliation.

perturbation.original_text: I understand that violations of this Agreement may result in re vocation of my user privileges and/or disciplinary action, up to and including termination of my employment, affiliation or association with FAU, and that FAU may seek any civil or crimin al recourse and/or equitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand that violations of this Agreement may result in re vocation of my user privileges and/or disciplinary action, up to and including termination of my employment, affiliation or association with FAU. FAU will not seek any civil or crimin al recourse and/or equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by stating that FAU will not seek any civil or criminal recourse. The original text stated that FAU may seek any civil or criminal recourse and/or equitable relief, in case the user violates the agreement. This is an in-text contradiction because it's not clear if FAU is allowed to seek or not civil or criminal actions. This contradicts the enforcability of the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I understand that violations of this Agreement may result in re vocation of my user privileges and/or disciplinary action, up to and including termination of my employment, affiliation or association with FAU. FAU will not seek any civil or crimin al recourse and/or equitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that FAU 'will not seek any civil or criminal recourse and/or equitable relief', while the original text stated that FAU 'may seek any civil or criminal recourse and/or equitable relief.' This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text denies an action that the original text allowed. The provided 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the 'Changed Text', so there is an in-text contradiction, because one clause is denying what it also allows, making the Agreement's enforceability unclear, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: I understand that my User ID and password are confidential, may only be used by m e, that I am responsible for safekeeping my password, that I am also responsible for any act ivity initiated by my User ID and password, and that in certain circumstances my User ID and pass word may be equivalent to my legal signature.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand that my User ID and password are confidential, may only be used by m e, but I am not responsible for safekeeping my password, that I am also not responsible for any act ivity initiated by my User ID and password, and that in certain circumstances my User ID and pass word may be equivalent to my legal signature.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the user's responsibilities related to their User ID and password. It directly contradicts the original statement that the user is responsible for safekeeping their password and for activities initiated by their User ID, This undermines the security of the system and the user's accountability for their actions under their account.
perturbation.location: First set of bullet points, sixth bullet point
perturbation.contradicted_location: First set of bullet points, sixth bullet point
perturbation.contradicted_text: I understand that I will be given a unique User ID and password to access electronic health, operational, proprietary, student or employee or other confiden tial information. I understand that my User ID and password are confidential, may only be used by m e, but I am not responsible for safekeeping my password, that I am also not responsible for any act ivity initiated by my User ID and password, and that in certain circumstances my User ID and pass word may be equivalent to my legal signature. If I suspect that my User ID or password has b een compromised, I should immediately contact FAU’s Office of Information Technology (“FA U OIT”).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original statement regarding the user's responsibility for safekeeping their password and actions initiated by their User ID. The original text states the user *is* responsible, while the changed text states the user is *not* responsible. This creates a clear and direct contradiction with the contradicted text, as the user is expected to understand their responsibilities regarding their User ID and Password.

perturbation.original_text: I agree to practice good workstation security me asures on any computing device that uses or accesses a FAU information system. Good security m easures include, but are not {00366300; 1 } 2 limited to, maintaining physical security of electronic devices , never leaving a device unattended while in use, and adequately shielding the screen from unauthor ized viewing by others.
perturbation.changed_text: I agree to practice good workstation security me asures on any computing device that uses or accesses a FAU information system. Good security m easures include, but are not {00366300; 1 } 2 limited to, optional security of electronic devices , can leave a device unattended while in use, and shielding the screen from unauthor ized viewing by others is optional.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding security measures. By replacing 'maintaining physical security' with 'optional security' and stating leaving the device unattended is allowed, it contradicts the idea of 'good workstation security measures' mentioned earlier. This contradicts the security aspect of the agreement.
perturbation.location: First set of bullet points, seventh bullet point
perturbation.contradicted_location: First set of bullet points, seventh bullet point
perturbation.contradicted_text: I have no expectation of privacy when using FAU information sys tems. FAU shall have the right to record, audit, log, and/or monitor access to or use of its i nformation systems that is attributed to my User ID. I agree to practice good workstation security me asures on any computing device that uses or accesses a FAU information system. Good security m easures include, but are not {00366300; 1 } 2 limited to, optional security of electronic devices , can leave a device unattended while in use, and shielding the screen from unauthor ized viewing by others is optional.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the concept of 'good workstation security measures' by allowing 'optional security' and permitting devices to be left unattended. The original text, and the contradicted text, both emphasize the importance of security. The modified text undermines the user's obligation to maintain security, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I understand that upon terminati on of my employment/affiliation /association with FAU, I will immediately return or destroy, as appropriate, any FAU Confiden tial Information, Restricted Data and Sensitive Data in my possession. I understand that my confi dentiality obligations under this Agreement will continue after the termination of this Agreement and after termination of my employment, affiliation or association with FAU.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand that upon terminati on of my employment/affiliation /association with FAU, I will keep any FAU Confiden tial Information, Restricted Data and Sensitive Data in my possession for reference in any future employment. I understand that my confi dentiality obligations under this Agreement will not continue after the termination of this Agreement and after termination of my employment, affiliation or association with FAU.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding what happens after employment at FAU is terminated. The original text ensures that confidentiality will continue after the user has left. The modified agreement states that users will keep any FAU confidential information, Restricted Data and Sensitive Data. This undermines the purpose of the agreement and the protection of sensitive information.
perturbation.location: First set of bullet points, tenth bullet point
perturbation.contradicted_location: First set of bullet points, tenth bullet point
perturbation.contradicted_text: I understand that upon terminati on of my employment/affiliation /association with FAU, I will keep any FAU Confiden tial Information, Restricted Data and Sensitive Data in my possession for reference in any future employment. I understand that my confi dentiality obligations under this Agreement will not continue after the termination of this Agreement and after termination of my employment, affiliation or association with FAU.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The first sentence states that the user will keep FAU Confidential Information, Restricted Data, and Sensitive Data, while the second sentence states that confidentiality obligations will not continue after termination. This is a clear in-text contradiction as the user cannot both keep confidential information and have no confidentiality obligations regarding it, making the terms incompatible and unenforceable.
